contribution.title: Penrose singularity theorem
contribution.description: Proved that, under physically reasonable energy conditions, gravitational collapse must lead to a spacetime singularity.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Justifies treating black-hole interiors as places where the classical theory breaks down. Bounds the model_status of interior claims.

contribution.title: Penrose process
contribution.description: Energy extraction from a rotating black hole via the ergosphere.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Future work on Kerr energetics and accretion-disk models.

contribution.title: Penrose diagrams
contribution.description: Conformal compactifications used to visualize the global causal structure of spacetimes.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Useful for visualization_metaphor modules and for reasoning about horizons.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Global topological and causal arguments rather than local coordinate computations.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What does the global causal structure of this spacetime look like?
Does an energy condition fail anywhere?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Conflating mathematical idealizations (closed trapped surface, asymptotic flatness) with astrophysical realities without saying so.

reference.citation: Penrose, R. (1965). Gravitational collapse and space-time singularities. Phys. Rev. Lett. 14, 57.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Reasoning about black-hole interiors, energy extraction, cosmological singularities, or whether a claim is global vs local.
how_to_apply.cautions: His later speculative work (cyclic conformal cosmology, Orch-OR consciousness) is not validated physics. Label any incursion of those ideas as speculative_extension.
